medications.drug_name: systemic antibiotics (anti-streptococcal; IV if toxic) — via in-person/ED
medications.indication_rationale: Erysipelas is streptococcal; systemic antibiotics urgently, with IV therapy for facial/toxic disease decided in person.
medications.dose_route_frequency: e.g., amoxicillin-clavulanate 875/125 mg twice daily (as per source note; IV per severity)

medications.drug_name: home oral antibiotics with routine follow-up as sole plan
medications.indication_rationale: Inadequate for a toxic facial erysipelas near the orbit that cannot be examined remotely — needs urgent in-person/ED assessment.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Keep the head elevated to reduce facial swelling; spouse to watch for eye or neurological changes while arranging urgent assessment.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Elevation reduces oedema; a companion enables rapid escalation.

patient_education_points.point: This is a serious, fast-spreading facial skin infection with a high fever. Because it's on your face near your eyes and you're systemically unwell, you need to be assessed in person today — likely at hospital for antibiotics into a vein.
Keep your head elevated on pillows to ease the swelling while you arrange to be seen.
If the redness reaches your eyelids, you get eye pain, double vision or pain moving your eyes, you become confused, or the fever worsens — call 000 immediately.

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Advancing border, systemic response, orbital/necrotising exclusion, antibiotic route.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Eye involvement, confusion, or worsening fever — call 000.

must_include_items: Recognise high-acuity facial erysipelas with systemic toxicity
Arrange urgent same-day in-person/ED assessment (likely IV antibiotics) — not remote oral management
Screen for/safety-net orbital cellulitis, necrotising fasciitis and sepsis
acceptable_alternatives: IV vs oral antibiotics decided in person by severity
errors_of_omission: Managing a toxic facial infection remotely with oral antibiotics only
errors_of_commission: Diagnosing a lupus malar rash and missing the infection
Reassuring and delaying with routine follow-up
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: HIGH-ACUITY CASE: failing to escalate a toxic facial erysipelas for urgent in-person/ED assessment, or missing the orbital/necrotising screen, is a critical failure.
